request.question: Introducing Sporomusa ovata into a bioelectrochemical system at a cathode potential of −400mV vs SHE will fix CO₂ into acetate at a rate of at least 150 mmol/L/day, outperforming current biocatalytic carbon capture benchmarks by at least 20%

hypothesis.intervention: introducing Sporomusa ovata into a bioelectrochemical system at a cathode potential of −400mV vs SHE
hypothesis.measurable_outcome: rate of CO₂ fixation into acetate
hypothesis.readiness_rationale: Mechanistic rationale is missing; other fields are present but the mechanism is not specified.
clarifying_questions: What is the biological mechanism by which Sporomusa ovata fixes CO₂ into acetate?
hypothesis.literature_search_hint: Sporomusa ovata bioelectrochemical system CO₂ fixation acetate
hypothesis.original_hypothesis: Introducing Sporomusa ovata into a bioelectrochemical system at a cathode potential of −400mV vs SHE will fix CO₂ into acetate at a rate of at least 150 mmol/L/day, outperforming current biocatalytic carbon capture benchmarks by at least 20%

references.title: Effect of tungstate on acetate and ethanol production by the electrosynthetic bacterium Sporomusa ovata
references.relevance_note: This paper directly investigates the effect of various conditions on acetate production by Sporomusa ovata, which is the same biological system and intervention proposed in the hypothesis.

literature_qc.explanation: The retrieved paper [3] discusses the electrosynthetic capabilities of Sporomusa ovata, specifically focusing on acetate production, which aligns closely with the proposed intervention of introducing Sporomusa ovata into a bioelectrochemical system. The biological system is the same, and the experiment type of biocatalytic carbon capture is directly addressed in this context. Therefore, this paper provides a direct match for the hypothesis.
literature_qc.recommended_action: A paper matching this experiment was found in the scientific literature. Review it before proceeding. If this is intentional replication, state that explicitly. If not, differentiate your approach.

risks.mitigation: Confirm all materials, controls, and readiness criteria with a scientist before execution.

plan.objective: To determine the rate of CO₂ fixation into acetate by Sporomusa ovata in a bioelectrochemical system at a cathode potential of −400 mV vs SHE.
plan.experimental_design: The experiment will involve culturing Sporomusa ovata and introducing it into a bioelectrochemical system. The rate of CO₂ fixation into acetate will be measured and compared against current biocatalytic carbon capture benchmarks. The experimental setup will include controls to validate the results.
controls: Known acetate-producing Sporomusa ovata culture at −400 mV vs SHE.
Abiotic cathode at −400 mV vs SHE with sterile medium.

step_by_step_protocol.description:
DAY 0 (PREP) | 1 h | Reagent Preparation
Prepare 1 L of anaerobic growth medium containing 50 mM NaHCO3, 10 mM K2HPO4, 1 mM MgSO4, and 0.1 mM FeCl3 in distilled water, adjusting pH to 7.0 with HCl.
Expected outcome: Medium is prepared with pH 7.0 and is anaerobic.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 30 min | Reagent Preparation
Prepare 100 mL of 1 M sodium acetate solution in distilled water for use as a positive control.
Expected outcome: Sodium acetate solution is prepared and mixed thoroughly.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 1 h | Equipment Setup
Assemble the bioelectrochemical cell using a glass reactor with a working volume of 500 mL, ensuring all connections are airtight.
Expected outcome: Bioelectrochemical cell is assembled and leak-tested.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 1 h | Electrode Preparation
Clean the graphite cathode by sonication in 70% ethanol for 30 min, followed by rinsing with distilled water.
Expected outcome: Graphite cathode is cleaned and ready for use.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 4 h | Inoculation Preparation
Inoculate 50 mL of anaerobic growth medium with 5 mL of Sporomusa ovata culture (OD600 ~0.5) and incubate at 30°C for 4 h.
Expected outcome: Inoculated culture reaches an OD600 of approximately 0.5.

DAY 1 | 2 h | Electrode Conditioning
Condition the cathode by applying a potential of −400 mV vs SHE for 2 h in the anaerobic medium to stabilize the electrode.
Expected outcome: Cathode potential stabilizes at −400 mV vs SHE.

DAY 1 | 30 min | Inoculation
Transfer the inoculated Sporomusa ovata culture into the bioelectrochemical cell containing 500 mL of anaerobic growth medium.
Expected outcome: Sporomusa ovata is successfully introduced into the bioelectrochemical cell.

DAY 1 | 1 h | CO₂ Introduction
Introduce CO₂ gas into the bioelectrochemical cell at a flow rate of 100 mL/min for 1 h to saturate the medium.
Expected outcome: Medium is saturated with CO₂.

DAY 1 | 5 min | Electrochemical Measurement
Set the potentiostat to maintain a constant potential of −400 mV vs SHE during the experiment.
Expected outcome: Potentiostat is set to maintain −400 mV vs SHE.

DAY 1 | 30 min | Sampling
Collect 5 mL samples from the bioelectrochemical cell every 6 h for 48 h to measure acetate concentration.
Expected outcome: Samples are collected at designated time intervals.

DAY 1 | 2 h | Acetate Quantification
Analyze acetate concentration in collected samples using HPLC with a C18 column at 30°C and a flow rate of 1 mL/min.
Expected outcome: Acetate concentration is quantified in each sample.

DAY 2 | 1 h | Data Collection
Record the acetate concentrations from HPLC analysis and calculate the rate of CO₂ fixation into acetate.
Expected outcome: Acetate production rates are calculated.

DAY 2 | 48 h | Control Comparison
Run the same procedure with abiotic control (sterile medium) at −400 mV vs SHE to establish baseline acetate production.
Expected outcome: Baseline acetate production is established for comparison.

DAY 3 | 1 h | Data Analysis
Perform statistical analysis using ANOVA to compare acetate production rates between Sporomusa ovata and control conditions.
Expected outcome: Statistical significance of acetate production rates is determined.

DAY 3 | 1 h | Result Interpretation
Interpret results to assess the effectiveness of Sporomusa ovata in CO₂ fixation compared to benchmarks.
Expected outcome: Conclusions drawn regarding the efficiency of Sporomusa ovata in acetate production.

DAY 3 | 2 h | Documentation
Document all experimental procedures, results, and analyses in a lab notebook and prepare a report.
Expected outcome: Comprehensive report on the experiment is completed.

DAY 3 | 1 h | Review Meeting
Conduct a review meeting with the research team to discuss findings and implications for future research.
Expected outcome: Research team discusses and evaluates experimental outcomes.

assumptions: Sporomusa ovata can effectively fix CO₂ into acetate at −400 mV vs SHE.
The bioelectrochemical system is stable and maintains the desired potential throughout the experiment.
Acetate production can be accurately quantified using HPLC.
decision_criteria: Proceed if acetate production rate ≥ 150 mmol/L/day.
Confirm statistical significance (p < 0.05) in acetate production compared to controls.
Ensure reproducibility of results in at least three independent trials.
reproducibility_notes: Use the same batch of Sporomusa ovata for all trials.
Calibrate the HPLC system before each analysis.
Maintain consistent CO₂ flow rates during all experiments.

validation.primary_endpoint: Post-thaw viability percentage at immediate and 24h checkpoints.
validation.success_threshold: at least 150 mmol/L/day improvement over comparator in replicate-confirmed data.
failure_conditions: No meaningful viability gain in replicate-confirmed runs.
Intervention increases delayed viability loss at 24h.
validation.suggested_statistical_comparison: Two-group comparison against comparator using t-test or Mann-Whitney based on normality.
validation.minimum_replicates_or_design_note: Minimum three biological replicates before execution-level claim.

cro_ready_brief.objective: To determine the rate of CO₂ fixation into acetate by Sporomusa ovata in a bioelectrochemical system at a cathode potential of −400 mV vs SHE.
scope_of_work: Execute pilot-first HeLa cryopreservation comparison between DMSO baseline and trehalose arm.
Deliver replicate-confirmed viability analysis and mitigation traceability report.
cro_ready_brief.sample_count: At least 3 biological replicates per arm
cro_ready_brief.materials_responsibility: CRO to quote and confirm all materials before ordering.
questions_for_cro: Can you support controlled-rate freezing logs in each run?
Can you include replicate-level batch metadata in the final report?
